lede: A look at October 30 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Giuseppe Farina and Julia the Elder, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2020 Aegean Sea earthquake
events.desc: A magnitude 7.0 earthquake strikes the Aegean Sea between Greece and Turkey, triggering a tsunami. At least 119 people die mainly due to collapsed buildings.

events.title: European Union
events.desc: Sweden becomes the first European Union member state to officially recognize Palestine as an independent and sovereign state.

events.title: Quebec
events.desc: Quebec citizens narrowly vote (50.58% to 49.42%) in favour of remaining a province of Canada in their second referendum on national sovereignty.

events.title: El Salvador
events.desc: El Salvador and Honduras agree to put the border dispute fought over in 1969's Football War before the International Court of Justice.

events.title: Korean People's Army
events.desc: A squad of 120 North Korean Army commandos land in boats along a 25-mile long section of the eastern coast of South Korea in a failed attempt to overthrow the dictatorship of Park Chung Hee and bring about the reunification of Korea.

events.title: Luzzu
events.desc: A luzzu fishing boat overloaded with passengers capsizes and sinks in the Gozo Channel off Qala, Gozo, Malta, killing 23 of the 27 people on board.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Lt. Tony Fasson and Able Seaman Colin Grazier drown while taking code books from the sinking German submarine U-559.

events.title: The Holocaust
events.desc: Holocaust: Fifteen hundred Jews from Pidhaytsi are sent by Nazis to Bełżec extermination camp.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The Ottoman Empire signs the Armistice of Mudros with the Allies.

events.title: War of the Fourth Coalition
events.desc: War of the Fourth Coalition: Convinced that he is facing a much larger force, Prussian General von Romberg, commanding 5,300 men, surrenders the city of Stettin to 800 French soldiers.

births.name: John Adams
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 2nd President of the United States

births.name: Danielle Fong
births.line: Canadian entrepreneur, co-founder and chief scientist of LightSail Energy

deaths.text: Harry Mulisch, Dutch author, poet, and playwright
Gustav Ludwig Hertz, German physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Luigi Einaudi, Italian economist and politician, 2nd President of the Italian Republic
Henry Dunant, Swiss activist, founded the Red Cross, Nobel Prize laureate
Charles IX of Sweden, King of Sweden from 1604 to 1611

observances.text: Anniversary of the Declaration of the Slovak Nation (Slovakia)
Christian feast day: Blessed Dominic Collins (Catholic, Ireland, Society of Jesus)
Christian feast day: Blessed Maria Teresa of St. Joseph
